meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio (Advanced Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no portfolio. A Advanced-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS pricing to Solve no portfolio
content.seo_description: Learn how to master SaaS pricing strategy and overcome a lack of portfolio. An advanced case study using Antigravity and Claude Code for developers.
content.answer_block: Overcome a lack of portfolio by building a high-value SaaS Pricing Calculator. By automating complex subscription tiers and usage-based billing, you demonstrate immediate ROI to clients. Use Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code to refine business logic, proving you can ship production-ready, revenue-generating features instantly.
content.intro:
The cold reality of freelancing is that clients don't buy code; they buy confidence. When your portfolio is empty, you are invisible. You need to stop pitching 'websites' and start shipping 'revenue engines.' By building a sophisticated SaaS pricing model, you aren't just showing off UI—you are demonstrating that you understand the mechanics of their business.

This isn't about static tables. This is about building dynamic, logic-driven pricing modules that handle tiered subscriptions and usage quotas. With the combined power of Antigravity and Claude Code, you can architect a professional-grade feature in an afternoon that would take a traditional dev team a week to polish.

steps.title: Define the Architecture
steps.description: Establish a robust data schema that supports multi-tier pricing, including flat-rate and usage-based components.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, scalable data model for a SaaS pricing engine using TypeScript. Include types for 'SubscriptionTier', 'UsageMetric', and 'PricingFormula'. Ensure it supports dynamic billing cycles.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Initialize a new project structure with the generated pricing schema and standard best practices for SaaS billing modules.'

steps.title: Implement Pricing Logic
steps.description: Create the calculation engine that converts user inputs into accurate monthly or annual billing projections.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a robust calculation engine in TypeScript that handles tiered pricing discounts and prorated billing logic. Use clean, functional programming patterns.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/engine/calculator.ts --instruction 'Apply the pricing logic to this file and add unit tests covering edge cases like mid-month upgrades.'

steps.title: Build the Interactive UI
steps.description: Develop a high-conversion UI that makes complex pricing simple for the end-user to understand.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a responsive pricing toggle component using Tailwind CSS and React. Include smooth transitions between monthly and annual plans.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate the React pricing component and integrate it into the main dashboard view, ensuring mobile responsiveness.'

steps.title: Integrate Stripe-Ready Webhooks
steps.description: Simulate real-world Stripe webhook handling to show clients you understand the full payment lifecycle.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Draft a Node.js webhook handler for Stripe events, specifically focusing on 'customer.subscription.updated' and 'invoice.payment_succeeded'.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit --file src/api/webhook.ts --instruction 'Implement the stripe webhook handler with secure signature verification and error logging.'

steps.title: Final Performance Audit
steps.description: Use automated tools to ensure the pricing engine is performant and free of logic bugs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive test suite for the pricing engine, including performance benchmarks and security checks for input sanitization.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Execute the test suite and optimize the calculator logic for O(1) complexity where possible.'

pro_tips: Always visualize the 'Upgrade Path'—show clients how your pricing engine encourages upsells through dynamic tooltips.
Use 'Price Anchoring' in your UI design; highlight the mid-tier plan to guide user psychology toward higher conversion.
Document your code as if it's for a Fortune 500 team; clean documentation is a portfolio-level asset itself.
Include a 'Projections' feature that calculates annual savings for users to emphasize value over monthly cost.
Build a 'Sandbox' mode into your project so prospective clients can toggle settings and see live results without needing a Stripe account.
content.next_steps: Deploy your pricing engine to Vercel, attach a Loom video walkthrough to your portfolio site, and start pitching it as a 'Ready-to-Deploy' module for SaaS startups.
content.conversion_block: You now have a high-value asset that proves you can build complex, revenue-focused features. Stop waiting for the perfect client and start building the perfect proof-of-work. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
